title: Argyreia nitida
taxonomy: Argyreia nitida is classified within the family Convolvulaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as morning glories and bindweeds. This plant family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes. Convolvulaceae is a large and diverse family with over 2,000 species spread across the globe, showcasing a wide range of growth habits, leaf shapes, and flower types. Argyreia nitida is native to tropical regions, where it thrives in humid environments with suitable temperatures. As part of the Convolvulaceae family, Argyreia nitida shares characteristics with other species in this group, such as twining or climbing stems.
